home.h1: Emotion, intention and social meaning in spoken language
home.p1: We investigate how people convey feelings, attitudes, and intentions when they speak: information carried not only by language itself (what is said), but also by the voice and facial expressions accompanying it (how it is said).
home.p2: The lab's overarching goal is to understand how adults express emotion and intention in spoken communication, how listeners decode these signals, and the brain mechanisms involved. We also study social and cultural factors that shape speech communication, combining acoustic, behavioural, EEG/ERP and fMRI methods to test theoretical hypotheses.
home.pi_role_extra: Vice Dean, Institute of Language Sciences, SISU
home.pi_bio:
Dr. Xiaoming Jiang is Professor, Doctoral Supervisor, and Vice Dean at the Institute of Language Sciences, Shanghai International Studies University. He works on psycholinguistics and neurolinguistics, with a focus on training the next generation of researchers in these fields. Since 2021 he has been continuously listed among Elsevier's Highly Cited Chinese Researchers in Foreign Languages & Literature. His research received the Best Paper Award in Speech Communication from ISCA and EURASIP, and he has been selected for Shanghai's Young Top-Notch Talent Program and the Chenguang Scholar Program.

The lab investigates how emotion, intention, and social meaning are conveyed in spoken communication — across vocal-emotion encoding and decoding, neuropragmatics and social communication, AI voice perception, cross-linguistic and cross-cultural cognition, and applied neurolinguistics with brain–machine-interface foundations. We combine acoustic, behavioural, eye-tracking, EEG/ERP, and fMRI methods to study multimodal information processing in both healthy and clinical populations.

The lab welcomes curious students, postdoctoral researchers, and visiting scholars interested in psycholinguistics and neurolinguistics, and we are open to collaborations with universities, research institutes, and clinical partners worldwide. Please get in touch by email.

people.intro: Current members, alumni, and our network of key collaborators worldwide. Click an avatar to expand a member's details.

projects.intro: Our research is organized around several interlocking themes, all centred on how vocal communication conveys social and emotional meaning.

facilities.intro: Our research uses the Key Laboratory of Language Sciences and Multilingual Artificial Intelligence, a university-level lab at the Institute of Language Sciences, Shanghai International Studies University. The lab comprises 7 sub-labs (Speech, Eye-tracking, EEG, Behavioral, Child Language, Language Data Processing & Application, and Smart Learning), and we have access to a 3T MRI system at the university's Brain Imaging Center.

news.intro: We have ongoing openings for Ph.D., postdoctoral, Master's, undergraduate, and visiting positions, alongside international collaboration calls and academic activity announcements.

contact.location_p1: Prof. Jiang's office is in Bldg. 5, Rm. 156, on the Songjiang campus of Shanghai International Studies University. From central Shanghai, take Metro Line 9 to Songjiang University Town station, then transfer to the campus shuttle (about 15 min).
contact.location_p2: Visitors from abroad are encouraged to contact us in advance; we can help arrange accommodation and visa documents.
